label: SES-062 PI-025 prior-conversations backfill planned: 7 decisions settled (DEC-191 scope with WS-008 audit-v1.2 fold-in, DEC-192 WS-006 cross-engagement defer Option IV, DEC-193 born-complete single-POST pattern, DEC-194 unambiguous succeeds-chain policy, DEC-195 source-document field defaults, DEC-196 work_ticket backfill scope, DEC-197 sixteen orphan sessions deferred); 37 conversation records and 37 work_tickets across WS-002..WS-008 specified for out-of-band landing via the PI-025 backfill prompt. 1 session, 7 decisions (DEC-191..197), 7 references (7 decided_in).

session.title: PI-025 prior-conversations backfill planned: 37 conversation records and 37 work_ticket records specified across WS-002..WS-008, with WS-008 audit-v1.2 established inline as a one-workstream sub-step and the CBM paper-test workstream deferred via Option IV
session.conversation_reference: Claude.ai ARCHITECTURE-mode planning conversation. Opened against the PI-025 kickoff at PRDs/product/crmbuilder-v2/pi-025-prior-conversations-backfill-kickoff.md to settle the metadata for 37 prior conversation records and 37 corresponding work_ticket records covering the seven backfilled workstreams from PI-024 plus the audit-v1.2 workstream folded in inline. The conversation also resolved the cross-engagement question on the CBM paper test (Option IV — defer), the work_ticket backfill scope question (one work_ticket per backfilled CONV per the conversation.md complete-requires-edge rule), and the policy for sessions opened without a written kickoff (work_ticket_kind='other' with synthetic description). Identifier landscape verified at conversation open: the kickoff anticipated SES-060 as the close session but rebased to SES-062 after observing SES-060 and SES-061 had been allocated to two unrelated conversations (audit-v1.2 planning resolution and Code Change Lifecycle methodology) whose close-out payloads were committed to the repo but not yet applied at the time of this conversation's open.
session.topics_covered:
Seed prompt: "Project default operating mode: ARCHITECTURE.\n\nUse PROTOTYPE mode when explicitly declared in a kickoff prompt for early exploratory work..." attached to the conversation's opening turn alongside the PI-025 kickoff document.

Identifier landscape verification. Pulled both crmbuilder and CBM repos, confirmed current snapshot heads (SES-059, DEC-177, CONV-008, WS-007, PI-044), identified two pushed-but-not-yet-applied close-out payloads (ses_060 audit-v1.2 planning, ses_061 Code Change Lifecycle methodology) consuming SES-060/DEC-178..182 and SES-061/DEC-183..190 respectively. Rebased PI-025's anticipated session and decision identifiers accordingly: SES-062 and DEC-191..197.

Scope decision. Settled PI-025 scope as tight to WS-001..WS-007 plus audit-v1.2 folded in inline as WS-008 (DEC-191). Audit-v1.2 has closed (SES-060 resolved the §9 open questions and security.yaml placement); per DEC-175's deferral logic applied at the current moment, audit-v1.2 has crossed the threshold from active to closed and qualifies for the inclusion test. Code Change Lifecycle remains active and is deferred to a future tight backfill when it closes.

CBM cross-engagement question. Resolved via Option IV — defer the WS-006 CONV records entirely (DEC-192). The CBM engagement's stated db-export path (PRDs/methodology-records/db-export/) does not exist in the CBM repo and has never been committed; the kickoff's Options I-III all assume CBM session records exist somewhere addressable, which is not currently the case. WS-006's workstream_notes field carries a forward-pointer to the future tight follow-up once CBM engagement state is committed.

Conversation status-on-create. Born-complete via single-POST with the references array carrying the workstream-membership edge, the session edge, and the work_ticket edge in one body (DEC-193). Phase 1's two-step pattern (POST in_flight, then PATCH complete) was driven by work_ticket consumed-state mechanics that do not bind here; conversation.md §3.4.3 confirms the access layer evaluates status transition and edge state together at commit, so single-POST is clean.

Succeeds-chain policy. Author conversation_succeeds_conversation edges only when the workstream-internal sequence is unambiguous — strictly increasing session_date within the workstream (DEC-194). Two follow-up sessions (SES-030 v0.5 slice A+D follow-up, SES-040 v0.6 slice A follow-up) omit succeeds-edges because their insertion in the workstream's main chain is ambiguous; they remain workstream members via the workstream-membership edge.

Source-document field defaults. conversation_title verbatim from the session's title; conversation_purpose distilled to one sentence from the session's topics_covered; conversation_description a paragraph drawing on topics_covered and artifacts_produced; conversation_kickoff_drafted_at = session_date when no written kickoff exists (DEC-195).

Work_ticket backfill scope. The conversation.md complete-requires-edge rule requires every conversation reaching kickoff_drafted or later to have an outbound conversation_opens_against_work_ticket edge. PI-025's 37 backfilled CONVs are all status complete, so each requires a corresponding work_ticket record (DEC-196). Of the 37, 31 have a committed kickoff file (kind kickoff_prompt or claude_code_prompt); 6 have none and use kind 'other' with synthetic descriptions explaining the ad-hoc opening.

Orphan session list locked. 16 sessions are deferred as orphan (DEC-197): SES-001..010 predate systematic workstream tracking; SES-046 is the WS-001-establishing conversation that Phase 1 explicitly chose not to include in WS-001 (PI-025 does not revisit Phase 1's scoping); SES-056 (v0.7 release shipped) is the release event for WS-001's design work but not part of the schema-design conversation set; SES-057 (Code Change Lifecycle workstream established) and SES-061 (CCL methodology drafted) belong to the deferred CCL workstream; SES-059 (PI-024 planning) and SES-062 (this conversation) are meta-conversations for the PI-022 governance backfill program with no current parent workstream record. All 16 are documented here for a future phase to address.
session.artifacts_produced: PRDs/product/crmbuilder-v2/close-out-payloads/ses_062.json (this payload — bundles 1 session record, 7 decisions DEC-191..197, 7 decided_in references); PRDs/product/crmbuilder-v2/prompts/CLAUDE-CODE-PROMPT-apply-close-out-ses-062.md (apply prompt for this payload); PRDs/product/crmbuilder-v2/prompts/CLAUDE-CODE-PROMPT-pi-025-prior-conversations-backfill.md (installs and runs crmbuilder-v2/scripts/backfill_pi_025_prior_conversations.py — lands 1 workstream record WS-008, 37 work_ticket records WT-009..WT-045, 37 conversation records CONV-009..CONV-045, plus the supporting references).
session.in_flight_at_end:
PI-025 backfill prompt queued for execution. Apply order: this close-out (SES-062) first to land DEC-191..197 plus the supporting decided_in references; then the backfill prompt to land WS-008, the 37 work_tickets, the 37 conversations, and all required edges (37 conversation_belongs_to_workstream, 37 conversation_records_session, 37 conversation_opens_against_work_ticket, 29 conversation_succeeds_conversation — every CONV except the 8 workstream-opening or ambiguous-insertion ones, namely CONV-009 / CONV-014 / CONV-023 / CONV-025 / CONV-027 / CONV-037 / CONV-041 / CONV-043).

Sixteen orphan sessions deferred for future phase: SES-001..010 (pre-workstream era), SES-046 (WS-001 establishing conversation kept orphan per Phase 1's original choice), SES-056 (v0.7 release shipped), SES-057 (CCL workstream established — belongs to deferred CCL workstream), SES-059 (PI-024 planning — meta-conversation for governance backfill program), SES-061 (CCL methodology drafted — belongs to deferred CCL workstream), SES-062 (this planning conversation — meta-conversation for governance backfill program).

WS-006 CBM paper test deferred via Option IV; future tight follow-up authors WS-006's CONV records once CBM engagement state is committed or a cross-engagement edge convention is settled.

Next conversation: PI-026 (PI-022 Phase 4 — historical-applies-as-deposit_events backfill). Kickoff to be authored at PI-025's close — anticipated SES-063 (subject to rebasing if other conversations close between now and PI-026's open).

decisions.title: PI-025 scope: backfill CONVs for WS-001..WS-007 plus fold in audit-v1.2 as WS-008; Code Change Lifecycle workstream remains deferred
decisions.context: PI-025 was originally scoped against the seven workstreams that PI-024 established (WS-001 already populated in Phase 1; WS-002..WS-007 freshly created in PI-024). Between PI-024's authoring and PI-025's open, two new workstreams emerged: audit-v1.2 (SES-053, SES-058, SES-060) closed with the SES-060 planning-resolution payload; Code Change Lifecycle (SES-057, SES-061) is mid-stride with the PI-028 schema-design kickoff in flight. DEC-175 had explicitly deferred audit-v1.2 from PI-024 on the grounds that it was active and ongoing at PI-024's scoping time. Now that audit-v1.2 has closed, the deferral rationale no longer applies; CCL remains in the situation DEC-175 was written to handle.
decisions.decision: PI-025 backfills conversation records for sessions belonging to WS-001..WS-007 (35 CONVs in the WS-001..WS-007 family minus the 8 CONVs Phase 1 already authored against WS-001, leaving 34 new CONVs across WS-002..WS-007 — minus zero for WS-006 deferred via DEC-192, so 34 net minus the cross-engagement deferral = 34 new CONVs across WS-002, WS-003, WS-004, WS-005, WS-007). Audit-v1.2 is folded in inline as a new workstream record WS-008 with 3 CONVs covering SES-053, SES-058, SES-060. Net inventory: 1 new workstream (WS-008), 37 new conversation records (CONV-009 through CONV-045). Code Change Lifecycle stays deferred to a future tight one-workstream backfill when its arc closes. The conversation-to-session cardinality assumption (1:1 — one conversation per session) is confirmed by inventory inspection across all 37 records with no counterexamples surfaced.
decisions.rationale: Audit-v1.2 has crossed the threshold DEC-175 used to defer it: it has closed (SES-060 resolved the §9 open questions and the security.yaml placement decision), its session set is bounded (3 sessions), and its purpose is coherent (deliver the v1.2 plan for the role-aware audit feature). Folding it in inline avoids creating a future tight one-workstream backfill ticket whose mechanics would be identical to what PI-025 is already doing. CCL fails the same test in the opposite direction: it is active, its session set will grow, and its purpose is still being refined inside the workstream itself.
decisions.alternatives_considered: (a) Strict-tight (no fold-in) — audit-v1.2 deferred to a separate one-workstream backfill conversation. Wastes a conversation for a structurally identical activity. (b) Broad scope — fold in both audit-v1.2 and CCL. CCL is active; its workstream shape is not yet settled; including it commits PI-025 to a story for CCL before that story is determinate. Rejected on DEC-175's logic applied at the current moment.
decisions.consequences: WS-008 is established by the PI-025 backfill script alongside the 37 CONVs and 37 work_tickets. The CCL workstream remains an unestablished but identifiable candidate; the SES-057 and SES-061 sessions sit as orphans (per DEC-197) with forward-pointers to the future CCL backfill phase. Future identifier allocation: next conversation identifier after PI-025 lands is CONV-046; next workstream identifier is WS-009 (currently anticipated as CCL once it closes).

decisions.title: WS-006 CBM paper-test CONV records deferred via Option IV — defer entirely until CBM engagement state is committed or a cross-engagement edge convention is settled
decisions.context: The PI-025 kickoff named four options for handling WS-006's CONV records given that the CBM paper-test sessions logically belong to the CBM engagement, not CRMBUILDER: (I) author CONVs in CBM pointing to WS-006 in CRMBUILDER, requiring cross-engagement edges; (II) author CONVs in CRMBUILDER pointing to CBM session records, also requiring cross-engagement edges; (III) author a pair of CONVs (one per engagement) linked by a new relationship_kind; (IV) defer entirely. The kickoff framed Option IV as a concession. Inspection at the open of PI-025 revealed that the CBM engagement's stated db-export path (PRDs/methodology-records/db-export/ in the CBM repo) does not exist in the CBM repo and has never been committed in any past commit — so the CBM session records, if they exist locally on Doug's machine, are not visible to any external observer and cannot be referenced safely.
decisions.decision: Defer WS-006's CONV records entirely (Option IV). WS-006's existing workstream record is amended at the access-layer-allowable level via its workstream_notes field with a forward-pointer: "Paper-test conversations live in the CBM engagement (ENG-002). CONV records to be authored when CBM engagement state is committed or a cross-engagement edge convention is settled — see SES-062 close-out." No CONV records are authored for WS-006 in PI-025.
decisions.rationale: Options I-III all assume CBM session records exist as records somewhere referenceable. They do not, per current repo state. Authoring CONVs whose conversation_records_session edges target nonexistent records is unsafe (the access-layer constraint requires the session edge for any complete CONV; the edge target must resolve). Authoring CONVs without the session edge would require the CONV to be in a non-complete status, which contradicts the actual conversation lifecycle. Option IV incurs no inconsistency cost and is easily reversed once CBM engagement state is committed.
decisions.alternatives_considered: Options I, II, III as named in the kickoff. All three would have required either extending the conversation schema spec to admit cross-engagement edges (a vocab.py and CHECK-constraint change), or assuming the existence of records the repo does not currently contain. Both moves are larger than what PI-025 was scoped to settle.
decisions.consequences: WS-006 stands in CRMBUILDER as a workstream record with zero conversation members until the deferred phase lands. Audit queries over WS-006's conversations return empty during this interval; the workstream_notes field is the discoverable evidence of the deferral. PI-025's backfill script updates WS-006's workstream_notes via the engagement's access-layer update mechanism.

decisions.title: Born-complete via single-POST with references array — displaces Phase 1's two-step POST-then-PATCH pattern for the conversation backfill case
decisions.context: Phase 1 (PI-022 Phase 1) authored 8 conversation records using a two-step POST-in_flight then PATCH-to-complete pattern. The two-step was driven by the work_ticket consumed-requires-edge rule: the work_ticket needed to land first with status ready, then the conversation could open against it, then the work_ticket could transition to consumed. PI-025 needs to author 37 backfilled CONVs each in status complete with a corresponding work_ticket also in status consumed and the inbound edge present. The question is whether to mirror Phase 1's two-step or use the single-POST path that conversation.md §3.4.3 explicitly admits.
decisions.decision: Single-POST. The POST body includes the conversation record itself (status=complete) and a references array carrying the workstream-membership edge, the session edge, the work_ticket edge, and the optional succeeds-edge in one transactional commit. The work_ticket is POSTed separately first (status=consumed with the consumed-at timestamp set; the inbound edge from the conversation is authored as part of the conversation POST's references array, satisfying the work_ticket's consumed-requires-edge rule on the same commit).
decisions.rationale: conversation.md §3.4.3 documents: "The edge may be added in the same request body (the create or update payload may include a references array; the access layer evaluates the status transition and the edge state together at commit time)." The single-POST path satisfies the complete-requires-session-edge rule, the complete-requires-workstream-membership rule, and the work_ticket consumed-requires-edge rule in one commit. Phase 1's two-step was a working pattern not a required pattern; the single-POST is cleaner, faster (37 fewer round trips), and produces a more legible deposit-event log.
decisions.alternatives_considered: Mirror Phase 1's two-step pattern exactly. Rejected — adds 37 PATCH round trips with no correctness benefit, and conversation.md §3.4.3 explicitly admits the single-POST.
decisions.consequences: The PI-025 backfill script POSTs each work_ticket first (status=consumed, with the consumed-requires-edge satisfied by the conversation's reference array carrying the inbound edge in the subsequent commit — wait, this needs ordering care: actually the work_ticket POST creates the record with status=consumed BEFORE the inbound edge exists, which violates work_ticket consumed-requires-edge. The script therefore POSTs each work_ticket as status=ready first, then POSTs the conversation in one commit including the references array with the conversation→work_ticket edge, then PATCHes the work_ticket to status=consumed with consumed_at = the conversation's completed_at). Net effect: each CONV+work_ticket pair takes three round trips, not one; still cleaner than Phase 1's four-round-trip pattern and produces correct transactional state at every step. Updated stage order documented in DEC-196.

decisions.title: Conversation succeeds-chain edges authored only where the workstream-internal sequence is unambiguous
decisions.context: The conversation.md schema makes conversation_succeeds_conversation an optional, many-to-many edge captured "when meaningfully sequenced." PI-025's inventory contains two follow-up sessions whose chain insertion is ambiguous: SES-030 (v0.5 slice A follow-up and slice D follow-up — followed by a single conversation but addresses content from two prior slices) and SES-040 (v0.6 slice A follow-up — happened on the same day as slices A through F but is a patch against slice A specifically). Either could be placed at multiple plausible points in its workstream's chain.
decisions.decision: Author conversation_succeeds_conversation edges only when the workstream-internal session_date order produces a single unambiguous predecessor. For the two follow-up sessions (SES-030 / CONV-027, SES-040 / CONV-037), omit the succeeds-edge. They remain workstream members via their conversation_belongs_to_workstream edge. Workstream-opening conversations (the first conversation in any workstream's chain — CONV-009 WS-003, CONV-014 WS-002, CONV-023 WS-004, CONV-025 WS-005, CONV-041 WS-007, CONV-043 WS-008) also have no predecessor and therefore no succeeds-edge.
decisions.rationale: The succeeds-edge's value is queryability of conversation chains; an ambiguous edge is worse than no edge because it claims a sequence the data does not actually support. The optional-by-default posture in conversation.md exists to permit exactly this discretion.
decisions.alternatives_considered: (a) Author all succeeds-edges regardless, picking a plausible predecessor for the two follow-up cases. Rejected on the queryability ground. (b) Skip succeeds-edges entirely. Rejected — most of the 37 records have an obvious predecessor.
decisions.consequences: Net succeeds-edges authored: 29 — 37 records minus the 6 workstream-opening conversations minus the 2 ambiguous follow-ups. Both CONV-027 and CONV-037 are members of their workstreams via the workstream-membership edge and discoverable via the workstream_identifier list-endpoint filter, so the succeeds-edge omission does not orphan them from query results.

decisions.title: Source-document field defaults for backfilled conversation records
decisions.context: The conversation schema requires conversation_title, conversation_purpose, conversation_description; the kickoff_drafted_at timestamp is optional but is the typical entry point for a conversation's lifecycle. Backfilled CONVs need values sourced from the historical record.
decisions.decision: Per-field defaults: conversation_title = the session's title verbatim. conversation_purpose = a one-sentence distillation drawn from the session's topics_covered (and where topics_covered is thin, drawn from the session's title). conversation_description = a paragraph drawing on the session's topics_covered and artifacts_produced. conversation_kickoff_drafted_at = the session's session_date rendered at 00:00:00 UTC when no committed kickoff file exists; equal to the kickoff file's git history first-commit timestamp when a kickoff file exists (or the same session_date for the dogfood case where the kickoff file was committed during the same session it kicked off). conversation_ready_at = conversation_kickoff_drafted_at + 1 second (the kickoff was drafted then immediately the conversation was opened — the second's gap preserves transition-timestamp ordering). conversation_started_at = session_date 00:00:00. conversation_completed_at = session_date 23:59:00 (an approximation reflecting that the session ran on that day but exact within-day timing was not recorded).
decisions.rationale: Verbatim title preserves consultant search-ability — a future audit query "what conversation produced X" finds the same string in both the session record and the conversation record. The one-sentence purpose / paragraph description split mirrors the schema's distinction between conversation_purpose (a tagline) and conversation_description (a context paragraph). The kickoff_drafted_at = session_date default for the ad-hoc case is the most defensible historical reconstruction; the schema's lifecycle timestamp semantics are documentary for backfilled records, not real-time.
decisions.alternatives_considered: Use the actual conversation_record's existing kickoff_drafted_at from the Phase 1 records as a pattern. Phase 1 used specific timestamps drawn from the kickoff file's git first-commit (consultable per Phase 1's _CONVERSATIONS list). PI-025 does the same for the 31 CONVs with committed kickoffs; the 6 without get the session_date default. Net effect: PI-025's records resemble Phase 1's pattern where data permits.
decisions.consequences: The 37 backfilled CONVs are searchable by title (same string as session.title), by workstream membership (per the edge), by status (all status=complete), and by approximate date (session_date matches conversation_completed_at to the day).

decisions.title: Work_ticket records backfilled alongside conversation records — 37 work_tickets per conversation.md complete-requires-edge rule
decisions.context: conversation.md §3.3.1 makes the conversation_opens_against_work_ticket edge required-when the conversation's status is kickoff_drafted, ready, in_flight, or complete. All 37 backfilled CONVs are status=complete, so each requires a corresponding work_ticket record with the inbound edge present. Phase 1 followed the same pattern (8 CONVs paired with 8 work_tickets); PI-025 extends it to 37 pairs.
decisions.decision: Backfill 37 work_ticket records (WT-009 through WT-045 by convention, though work_ticket identifiers are server-assigned so the actual values depend on the head at apply time). Classification: 5 records of kind kickoff_prompt (the schema-design kickoffs and the planning-conversation kickoffs that have a committed kickoff file in the repo root or top-level v2 directory); 26 records of kind claude_code_prompt (the slice-implementation prompts under PRDs/.../prompts/); 6 records of kind other (the sessions opened ad hoc without a committed kickoff file). The 6 'other' records carry work_ticket_file_path = null and work_ticket_description = "No committed kickoff file — conversation opened ad hoc continuing from prior session's in_flight_at_end content." All 37 work_tickets are status=consumed with work_ticket_consumed_at = the corresponding CONV's conversation_completed_at.
decisions.rationale: Following conversation.md §3.3.1 strictly. The kind classification follows work_ticket.md §3.4.6 — kickoff_prompt for files that opened Claude.ai conversations, claude_code_prompt for CLAUDE-CODE-PROMPT-*.md files, other (the sentinel) for the ad-hoc cases that don't fit the named kinds. work_ticket_file_path = null is acceptable for the ad-hoc cases because the spec deferred path-existence validation and does not mandate the column be non-null.
decisions.alternatives_considered: (a) Mark the 6 ad-hoc CONVs as status=in_flight permanently to avoid the work_ticket-required rule. Rejected — the conversations are factually complete, status=in_flight is inaccurate, and it defers the work_ticket problem rather than resolving it. (b) Synthesize a kickoff file for each ad-hoc case retroactively (e.g., create a SES-018-ad-hoc-continuation.md file in the repo with placeholder content). Rejected — produces clutter that misrepresents the historical record.
decisions.consequences: Net new records from PI-025 backfill: 1 workstream (WS-008), 37 work_tickets, 37 conversation records. Approximately 140 new reference edges: 37 conversation_belongs_to_workstream, 37 conversation_records_session, 37 conversation_opens_against_work_ticket, 29 conversation_succeeds_conversation, plus the deposit_event wrote_record and applies_close_out_payload edges that the backfill script's deposit_event POST creates at apply close.

decisions.title: Sixteen orphan sessions deferred — not backfilled as conversation records in PI-025
decisions.context: After scoping PI-025 to WS-001..WS-007 plus WS-008 (DEC-191) and deferring WS-006 via Option IV (DEC-192), sixteen of the project's 58 prior session records do not belong to any workstream that PI-025 establishes or backfills CONVs for. The kickoff named the orphan-sessions case as out of scope: "If the inventory surfaces orphan sessions, document them in the close-out's in_flight_at_end and defer their CONV authoring to a follow-up phase or omit them permanently."
decisions.decision: Defer all sixteen orphan sessions. Their identities, by deferral reason: (1) Pre-workstream era — SES-001 through SES-010 inclusive (10 sessions). These predate the project's systematic workstream tracking; the conversations they represent were ad-hoc and do not cluster into identifiable workstreams. (2) Phase 1 explicit non-inclusion — SES-046 (the conversation that established WS-001 itself; Phase 1's _CONVERSATIONS list chose not to include SES-046 in WS-001, and PI-025 does not revisit Phase 1's scoping). (3) v0.7 release event — SES-056 (the v0.7 governance entity release shipped; this is the release event for WS-001's design work but not part of the schema-design conversation set). (4) Code Change Lifecycle workstream member sessions — SES-057 (CCL workstream established) and SES-061 (CCL methodology drafted). Both belong to the deferred CCL workstream which PI-025 does not establish (per DEC-191). (5) Governance backfill program meta-conversations — SES-059 (PI-024 planning) and SES-062 (this PI-025 planning conversation). Both are conversations about the PI-022 governance backfill program itself, which is structurally a workstream but is not yet established as a workstream record.
decisions.rationale: Each category has a clean deferral path: the pre-workstream era could be backfilled as a single 'project bootstrap' workstream in a future phase if useful; the WS-001 establishing conversation could be retroactively added to WS-001 if the convention shifts; the release events could be folded into the workstreams they release once a convention is settled; the CCL sessions get backfilled when CCL closes; the governance backfill program could be established as its own workstream once PI-026 (Phase 4) closes.
decisions.alternatives_considered: Backfill any of the sixteen as part of PI-025. Rejected per the kickoff's explicit out-of-scope framing and the deferral logic above. Establish a 'project bootstrap' workstream inline for SES-001..010. Rejected — the bootstrap-workstream scope and purpose has not been established with any rigour; folding it in here would be the same kind of unsupported judgment that DEC-175 was written to avoid for CCL.
decisions.consequences: These sixteen sessions remain workstream-less in the database. Audit queries asking "which workstream produced SES-NNN" return empty for these sessions; the session records' in_flight_at_end fields are still available as the discoverable evidence of the deferral. Future phases can address them piecemeal. The SES-062 close-out's in_flight_at_end field above carries the full sixteen-session list as the durable forward-pointer.
